name: Carnival of Cologne
dates: Starts on Weiberfastnacht for five days, main parade on Rosenmontag (Rose Monday)
description: One of Germany's most famous celebrations featuring a 7km parade with 10,000 participants throwing candy and the traditional figures of the prince, virgin, and farmer.

name: Carnival in Düsseldorf
dates: Peak on Rose Monday and the following days
description: A very colorful carnival featuring a 5km long Rose Monday parade with over 60 decorated carriages, 80 groups on foot, and 41 orchestras.

name: Mainz’s Carnival
dates: Last week of celebrations, main parade on Rose Monday
description: Known for its traditional 'Helau' greeting and a 6.5km long parade that is often broadcasted on German television.

name: Fasching
dates: Last days of Fasching, especially the weekend before Faschingsdienstag
description: Traditional Bavarian carnival featuring masquerade parades, singing, dancing concerts, Fasching doughnuts, and the traditional 'dance of the market women' at the Viktualienmarkt.
